control.name: The Control Plane
control.profile: High network I/O, low CPU, low RAM.
control.justification: This is the high-speed nervous system. It dictates traffic flow and maintains ephemeral state, operating flawlessly on low-power devices. INVARIANT: The runner service is included here explicitly so the Control Node can host the low-latency Judge Model locally, even in Tier 2 deployments.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: GMKtec Gaming Mini PC K8 Plus
hardware_recommendations.specs: AMD Ryzen 7 8845HS (8C/16T, up to 5.1GHz), 32GB DDR5 RAM, 1TB PCIe 4.0 M.2 SSD, Dual NIC 2.5G, HDMI 2.1, USB4, Oculink.
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Excellent network bridging with dual 2.5GbE; incredible price-to-performance ratio for a gateway node.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: The 32GB RAM ceiling is extremely tight if attempting to run memory-heavy background tasks on the same node.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Minisforum MS-01
hardware_recommendations.specs: Intel Core i9-13900H, up to 64GB RAM, Dual 10GbE SFP+, Dual 2.5GbE
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Unmatched homelab networking with dual 10GbE and 2.5GbE ports; provides massive routing throughput across the Tailscale mesh.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Intel H-series chips can run hot in tiny chassis, potentially leading to thermal throttling under sustained load.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Protectli Vault VP4670
hardware_recommendations.specs: Intel Core i7, 6x 2.5GbE Ports, Fanless Design
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Engineered specifically for 24/7 routing and firewall proxying; fanless design ensures zero dust intake and absolute acoustic silence.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Older generation CPU provides lower raw compute for non-routing tasks; RAM capacity is generally more limited.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Supermicro SYS-E300-9D
hardware_recommendations.specs: Intel Xeon D-2123IT, ECC Memory Support, Quad 1GbE, Dual 10GbE, IPMI
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Enterprise-grade edge server with IPMI for remote out-of-band management; ECC memory absolutely prevents routing table corruption.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Significantly more expensive than consumer equivalents; active cooling fans can be excessively loud in a home environment.

compute.name: The Compute Plane
compute.profile: Extreme GPU VRAM, maximum memory bandwidth, high power draw.
compute.justification: LLM inference requires dedicated PCIe acceleration. It must be isolated to prevent VRAM starvation from impacting other critical services.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Apple Mac Studio M5 Ultra
hardware_recommendations.specs: Projected up to 36-core CPU, 80-core GPU, ~1.2 TB/s Memory Bandwidth, 128GB+ Unified Memory.
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Unparalleled unified memory bandwidth (~1.2 TB/s) bypasses PCIe bottlenecks, enabling massive RAG payload evaluation faster than multi-GPU rigs.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Entirely locked ecosystem; no possibility of internal hardware expansion or VRAM upgrades after purchase.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Custom Multi-GPU Workstation
hardware_recommendations.specs: AMD Threadripper PRO, 4x NVIDIA RTX 4090 (96GB total VRAM), 256GB ECC RAM
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Achieves substantially higher tokens-per-second generation speeds than Apple Silicon via Tensor Parallelism across dedicated GDDR6X memory.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Extremely high power draw, massive physical footprint, and complex cooling requirements.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Apple Mac Pro (M-Series Ultra)
hardware_recommendations.specs: M2/M3 Ultra (up to 192GB Unified Memory), 800 GB/s bandwidth, PCIe expansion slots.
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Uses the exact same silicon as the Mac Studio but placed in a massive thermal enclosure to completely prevent thermal throttling.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Severe Apple Tax for the enclosure; internal PCIe slots cannot be used to add discrete NVIDIA GPUs for inference.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Lambda Tensorbook
hardware_recommendations.specs: Intel Core i7, NVIDIA RTX 3080/4080 (16GB), 64GB RAM, 1TB NVMe
hardware_recommendations.strengths: A portable, validated, Linux-native stack specifically tuned out-of-the-box for PyTorch and local LLM inference.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Laptop form factor restricts maximum VRAM (16GB limit), forcing reliance on smaller 8B/14B models or heavy quantization.

archive.name: The Archive Plane
archive.profile: Massive RAM capacity, extreme disk IOPS (NVMe), heavy storage.
archive.justification: This plane manages all 'Trusted Data'. Vector databases ingest massive streams of data and require enormous RAM. Keeping these safe from untrusted execution workloads is a strict SRE requirement.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: GMKtec EVO-X2 AI Mini PC
hardware_recommendations.specs: AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 (16C/32T, up to 5.1GHz, Radeon 8060S 40 Cores iGPU), 128GB LPDDR5X 8000MHz Quad Channel RAM, 2TB PCIe 4.0 SSD, Dual 2.5G LAN, WiFi 7, BT 5.4, USB4.
hardware_recommendations.strengths: 128GB RAM provides a massive `shared_buffers` cache, allowing PostgreSQL to keep entire vector indexes in memory for sub-millisecond semantic recall.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Lacks ECC memory, making it vulnerable to silent bit-flips corrupting the HNSW index graph over long uptimes.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: System76 Thelio Major
hardware_recommendations.specs: AMD Threadripper PRO, up to 512GB ECC DDR5, multiple U.2/NVMe slots
hardware_recommendations.strengths: ECC memory support prevents database corruption; massive internal bays allow creating striped RAID arrays for high-speed telemetry logs.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: High upfront cost and large physical footprint compared to mini-PCs.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Dell Precision 7960 Tower
hardware_recommendations.specs: Intel Xeon W-3400/3500 series, up to 4TB ECC RAM, front-accessible NVMe flex-bays
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Enterprise workhorse with hot-swappable drives, allowing you to easily scale your Context Lake into the hundreds of terabytes seamlessly.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Extremely expensive enterprise markup; proprietary motherboard/PSU form factors limit future DIY upgrades.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Custom Asrock Rack EPYC 8004 'Siena' Server
hardware_recommendations.specs: AMD EPYC 8004/4004 series, 96 PCIe Gen 5 lanes
hardware_recommendations.strengths: 96 PCIe Gen 5 lanes allow attaching dozens of NVMe drives directly to the CPU without chipset bottlenecks, achieving unparalleled log ingestion speeds.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Requires custom DIY server assembly and specialized cooling solutions.

execution.name: The Execution Plane
execution.profile: Heavy multi-core CPU, highly dynamic and unpredictable RAM usage.
execution.justification: This plane executes volatile, untrusted code. It is an operational hazard physically isolated to ensure that a rogue task cannot crash the Control Plane or corrupt the Data Plane.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: GMKtec EVO-X2 AI Mini PC
hardware_recommendations.specs: AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 (16C/32T, up to 5.1GHz, Radeon 8060S 40 Cores iGPU), 128GB LPDDR5X 8000MHz Quad Channel RAM, 2TB PCIe 4.0 SSD, Dual 2.5G LAN, WiFi 7, BT 5.4, USB4.
hardware_recommendations.strengths: 16 cores and 128GB RAM securely isolate dozens of concurrent `gVisor` sandboxes without starving the host OS.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Mobile processor cooling constraints may cause it to temporarily throttle under massive, sustained CI/CD parallel compilation workloads.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Lenovo ThinkStation P360 Ultra
hardware_recommendations.specs: Intel Core i5/i9 (desktop class), up to 128GB ECC RAM, 4-liter chassis
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Fits a full desktop-class 125W CPU into a tiny 4-liter chassis, offering significantly higher single-core turbo frequencies to accelerate Python code compilation and Docker image builds.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Custom form factor makes part replacement difficult; active cooling can get extremely loud under load.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: Minisforum BD790i
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Massive 3D V-Cache and blistering fast burst performance for rapidly spawning and destroying dozens of concurrent, ephemeral `gVisor` sandboxes.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: It is only a motherboard/CPU combo, requiring the user to build a custom ITX chassis, source RAM, storage, and a power supply.

hardware_recommendations.name: Example: System76 Thelio Mega
hardware_recommendations.specs: AMD Ryzen Threadripper 7000 (up to 64+ cores), up to 256GB ECC RAM
hardware_recommendations.strengths: Provides the raw 64+ hardware cores required to prevent thread contention when running massive parallel swarms (e.g., 50 agents scraping concurrently) natively under Linux.
hardware_recommendations.weaknesses: Massive cost and power draw for sandboxing workloads, usually overkill unless the agent swarm is operating at an enterprise scale.
